title: Users per plan
prompt:
How many users are on each **plan**?

Two columns: `plan`, `n_users`. Order by `n_users` descending.

Refresher goals: `COUNT(*)`, `GROUP BY`.
starter_code:
-- Tables: users, trips, payments, content, sessions_watch
-- Write your query below
reference_sql: SELECT plan, COUNT(*) AS n_users FROM users GROUP BY plan ORDER BY n_users DESC
hint_notes: Plain GROUP BY + COUNT(*). If they SELECT a non-grouped column, teach the GROUP BY rule.
